2604.05775.title: PhageBench: Can LLMs Understand Raw Bacteriophage Genomes?
2604.05775.paper_summary: Bacteriophages, often referred to as the dark matter of the biosphere, play a critical role in regulating microbial ecosystems and in antibiotic alternatives. Thus, accurate interpretation of their genomes holds significant scientific and practical value. While general-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at understanding biological texts, their ability to directly interpret raw nucleotide sequences and perform biological reasoning remains underexplored. To address this, we introduce PhageBench, the first benchmark designed to evaluate phage genome understanding by mirroring the workflow of bioinformatics experts. The dataset contains 5,600 high-quality samples covering five core tasks across three stages: Screening, Quality Control, and Phenotype Annotation. Our evaluation of eight LLMs reveals that general-purpose reasoning models significantly outperform random baselines in phage contig identification and host prediction, demonstrating promising potential for genomic understanding. However, they exhibit significant limitations in complex reasoning tasks involving long-range dependencies and fine-grained functional localization. These findings highlight the necessity of developing next-generation models with enhanced reasoning capabilities for biological sequences.
2604.05775.paper_summary_zh: 細菌噬菌體，通常被稱為生物圈的暗物質，在調節微生物生態系統和抗生素替代品中扮演著關鍵角色。因此，準確解讀它們的基因組具有重要的科學和實用價值。雖然通用型大型語言模型（LLMs）在理解生物學文本方面表現出色，但它們直接解釋原始核苷酸序列和進行生物學推理的能力仍然未被充分探索。為了解決這個問題，我們介紹了PhageBench，這是第一個旨在通過模擬生物信息學專家的工作流程來評估噬菌體基因組理解的基準。該數據集包含5600個高質量樣本，涵蓋三個階段的五個核心任務：篩選、質量控制和表型註釋。我們對八個LLMs的評估顯示，通用推理模型在噬菌體連接體識別和宿主預測方面顯著優於隨機基準，顯示出對基因組理解的良好潛力。然而，它們在涉及長距離依賴和細粒度功能定位的複雜推理任務中表現出顯著的局限性。這些發現突顯了開發具有增強推理能力的下一代模型以處理生物序列的必要性。
2604.05775.author: Yusen Hou et.al.
2604.05775.authors: Yusen Hou, Weicai Long, Haitao Hu, Houcheng Su, Junning Feng, Yanlin Zhang
